hints.Diligentibus: 'Diligentibus' is a present participle in the dative — 'for those who love'
hints.Deum: 'Deum' is a noun — the object of the participle
hints.omnia: 'omnia' is a pronoun/adjective meaning 'all things' — the subject

hints.to cooperate against: 'cooperantur' means to work together positively, not against

hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'cooperabantur' — 'cooperantur' lacks the -ba- marker

hints.operor, operari, operatus sum: These are the principal parts of 'operari' (to work) — the base verb without the co- prefix
hints.labor, labi, lapsus sum: These are the principal parts of 'labi' (to slip/fall), a different deponent
hints.conor, conari, conatus sum: These are the principal parts of 'conari' (to try), a different deponent

hints.Perfect passive participle: Perfect passive participle of 'diligere' would be 'dilectis' in dative plural
hints.Gerundive: Gerundive of 'diligere' would be 'diligendis' in dative plural
hints.Finite verb: 'Diligentibus' has a participial -nt- stem with a dative plural ending

hints.Ablative: 'Diligentibus' could be ablative plural (same form), but the dative of advantage fits — all things work together for those who love God

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Deus' — 'Deum' with -um ending is accusative

hints.Accusative: 'omnia' could be accusative neuter plural, but here it's the subject of 'cooperantur'

translation: All things work together for those who love God
